description: At a meeting, someone listens to the discussion and takes part in the decision. 'pawela' is active involvement in an activity or unfolding process; being present nearby is not enough. A person can participate without agreeing with the outcome or adding a separate contribution.
articulatory_notes: Close the lips for unvoiced 'p' and release them into open central unrounded 'a'. Round the lips and raise the back of the tongue for voiced 'w', then move forward and unround into stressed middle-height 'e'. Touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for voiced 'l', let air pass along its sides, and finish with open central 'a'.
usage_notes: The activity or process commonly appears in a locative phrase with 'mua'. The event-noun rule supplies participation. 'ruweli' (access) may end at reaching or using something, 'wemari' (member) is a recognised status, and 'shorema' (contribute) adds something to shared work.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Participatory practice gives affected people a way to enter the work itself. The word still leaves open whether access was practical and whether anyone listened.

semantic_domains.activity: taking an active part in an event or process
semantic_domains.community: participating in shared work or collective decisions
